methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $300M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $350M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

data.narrative:
GrayMatter Robotics, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ariyan Kabir and Brual Shah, is evaluated against a comparable universe of five private peers at the same stage (Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, Fairmat, and 1X Technologies) and one public benchmark. The company has raised $70M+ to date against a current post-money valuation of $300M+.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the last post-money valuation of $300M, implying $570M. Peer-median analysis across the five comparable companies yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $350M to $10.0B. The wide dispersion reflects differentiation in robotics subsector positioning and capital intensity; 1X Technologies at $10.0B represents an outlier.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $484M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables justify valuations substantially above current post-money. Key uncertainties include the degree to which GrayMatter's unit economics and go-to-market traction align with higher-quartile peers versus the broader median, and sector-specific risk around manufacturing automation adoption cycles.
